scenario.title: Proactive spend guardrails
scenario.stem: A logistics software company has had several surprise cloud-spend spikes after development teams launched temporary resources and forgot to remove them. Finance wants proactive alerts and optional automated actions when spending crosses thresholds, instead of waiting for end-of-month review. The director wants enforceable budget guardrails rather than just another dashboard. Which AWS service is the best fit?

options.label: AWS Budgets
options.rationale: It supports budgets, alerts, and budget actions around thresholds.

options.label: AWS Cost Explorer
options.rationale: It is strong for analysis and forecasting, but it is not the primary service for threshold alerts and budget actions.

options.label: AWS Pricing Calculator
options.rationale: It estimates future architecture cost, but it does not monitor live thresholds.

options.label: Amazon CloudWatch Logs
options.rationale: It stores logs, not budget thresholds.

answer.explanation: AWS Budgets is the best fit because it is built for threshold-based budget alerts and budget actions, which directly addresses proactive cost guardrails.
why_others_fail: Cost Explorer helps analyze and forecast spend, but it is not the main threshold-guardrail service.
Pricing Calculator estimates proposed architectures rather than enforcing live spend controls.
CloudWatch Logs is unrelated to billing guardrails.

sources.snippet_markdown: AWS Budgets best practices include setting budgets, understanding update frequency, and configuring budget alerts and actions. Budget actions also depend on the right IAM and notification permissions to run as intended.

quality.distractor_rule: All distractors are real AWS cost or observability services that miss the alert-and-action requirement.
anti_patterns_checked: all distractors are plausible but fail one requirement

scenario.title: Spend trend analysis and forecasting
scenario.stem: A public sector agency wants to analyze recent spend trends, compare usage over time, and forecast future cloud costs before next quarter's budget review. Finance wants a visual investigation tool rather than a one-time estimate and needs enough history to discuss cost movement with leadership. Which AWS service best fits?

options.label: AWS Cost Explorer
options.rationale: It is built for cost and usage analysis, historical review, and forecasting.

options.label: AWS Budgets
options.rationale: It is great for thresholds and alerts, but it is not the primary exploratory analysis tool.

options.label: AWS Pricing Calculator
options.rationale: It estimates planned architectures rather than analyzing actual spend history.

options.label: AWS Support Center
options.rationale: It manages support interactions, not cost analysis.

answer.explanation: AWS Cost Explorer is the best fit because it supports interactive cost and usage analysis, historical lookback, and forward forecasting for budgeting discussions.
why_others_fail: Budgets helps with thresholds and alerts, but it is not the main trend-analysis tool.
Pricing Calculator models future designs rather than actual historical spend.
Support Center is not a billing analytics service.

section_titles: Analyzing your costs and usage with AWS Cost Explorer
sources.snippet_markdown: AWS Cost Explorer enables teams to view and analyze cost and usage, look back up to 13 months, and forecast likely spend for the next 18 months. It helps identify areas that need further inquiry and understand cost trends over time.

deep_dive_sections: Analyzing your costs and usage with AWS Cost Explorer

quality.distractor_rule: All distractors are real AWS cost or support services that nearly fit but miss the historical-trend-analysis requirement.
anti_patterns_checked: all distractors are plausible but fail one requirement
